name: Aka Miso (Summer Miso Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the ichiban dashi in a 2 quart saucepan.
2. Set a sieve over the pan and use the back of a large spoon to rub the miso through the sieve, moistening it with some of the dashi from time to time to help force it through more easily.
3. Bring the soup to a simmer over moderate heat.
4. Remove from the heat and stir in a small pinch of MSG.
5. Pour the soup into bowls and add misoshiru no-me (miso soup garnish). Serve immediately. If the soup seems to be separating, stir to recombine it.
6. Miso soups are sweeter than other Japanese soups and are usually served toward the end of a formal Japanese meal.
